item.title: Example Talk
item.description:
Click on the Slides button above to view the built-in slides feature.

Slides can be added in a few ways:

  • Create slides using Wowchemy’s Slides feature and link using slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file
  • Upload an existing slide deck to static/ and link using url_slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file
  • Embed your slides (e.g. Google Slides) or presentation video on this page using shortcodes.

Further event details, including page elements such as image galleries, can be added to the body of this page.

item.title: Impact of Announced Wait Time Information on Emergency Department Overcrowding Mitigation: A Simulation Study
Beyond Crisis Management: Structural Reform for the Overcrowding in Hong Kong's Emergency Departments
Patient Sensitivity to Emergency Department Waiting Time Announcements
item.description:
What this paper is about

Many emergency departments publish expected waiting times to help patients choose where to seek care. Using over 1.3 million patient visits to Hong Kong public EDs, this study estimates how many patients actually respond to these announcements and how sensitive they are to the reported times. The findings inform the design of announcement systems that can reduce waiting times and the number of patients who leave without being seen.

item.title: Time-Varying Physician Productivity and Implications for Emergency Department Modeling and Staffing
Physician Scheduling in Case Managers Style Emergency Departments: Machine Learning-Aided Solution Approaches
Service-level Computation in Time-Varying Queueing System with Priorities: Application to Physician Staffing in the Emergency Department
Exact Algorithm and Machine Learning-Based Heuristic for the Stochastic Lot Streaming and Scheduling Problem
IIMT3635 Operations Management
item.description: Introduction to operations management, covering process analysis, capacity planning, quality management, and supply-chain fundamentals.

item.title: MAIB7013 Smart Manufacturing and Operations Management
item.description: Smart manufacturing technologies and their implications for operations strategy, planning, and execution.

item.title: MSBA7015 Service Operations
item.description: Operations management in service industries, with emphasis on service-system design, capacity management, and customer flow.

item.title: Dynamic Allocation of Time-Critical Resources in Disaster Response
MSBA7004 Operations Analytics
item.description: Analytic techniques for operational decisions, including process analysis, capacity planning, and continuous improvement methods.

item.title: Investigation of Time-dependent Physician Productivity and Application in Emergency Department Waiting Time Management
Assigning Priorities (or not) in Service Systems with Nonlinear Waiting Costs
The Impact of Emergency Department Crowding on Admission Decisions and Patient Outcomes
MSBA7015 Business Simulation
item.description: Hands-on introduction to simulation modeling for business decision making using @Risk and Simio.

item.title: Hong Kong RECAP: A Systematic Response Strategy for Novel Infectious Disease Pandemic
Scheduling of Physicians with Time-Varying Productivity Levels in Emergency Departments
On Scheduling a Two-Class Queue with Concave Waiting Cost
Optimal ICU Bed Allocation with Patient Health Evolution and Readmission
Priority Scheduling When Job Type Information Is Not Free
Impact of Emergency Department Waiting Time Announcements on Patient Behavior
PMBA6101 Operations Analytics
item.description: Data-driven operations analytics for MBA students, focusing on process analysis and operational decision making.

item.title: Allocation of Intensive Care Unit Beds in Periods of High Demand
item.description:
What this paper is about

When an intensive care unit (ICU) runs out of beds during a surge, hospitals must decide whether to admit a new patient to the ICU or transfer an existing patient to a general ward. This paper develops a Markov-chain model of patient health evolution and proposes admission/transfer policies that minimize the long-run mortality rate.

item.title: IIMT3636 Decision and Risk Analysis
item.description: Quantitative methods for analyzing business decisions under uncertainty.

item.title: Simulation-based Predictive Analytics for Dynamic Queueing Systems
Prioritization in Service Systems with Nonlinear Delay Costs
